content.full_text:
September 16, 1976

Lou Green  
Office of the City Attorney  
Civic Center  
250 Hamilton Avenue  
Palo Alto, CA 94301  

Dear Mr. Green:

Enclosed please find a copy of the Commission's opinion in response to a request from Mark Fontana, 2 FPPC Opinions 25 (No. 75-162, Feb. 4, 1976). As I indicated to you on the telephone, the opinion concludes that the term "proponent," as used in Government Code Section 84202, applies only to persons who circulate initiative, referendum or recall petitions.

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

Kenneth Finney  
Chief, Legal Division

KF:cjb  
Enclosure

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the term 'proponent' under Government Code Section 84202 applies to persons who circulate initiative, referendum, or recall petitions.

CONCLUSION: The term 'proponent' as used in Government Code Section 84202 applies only to persons who circulate initiative, referendum, or recall petitions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC Chief of Legal Division responds to the City Attorney of Palo Alto referencing a prior opinion (2 FPPC Opinions 25, No. 75-162) regarding the definition of 'proponent' under Government Code Section 84202, concluding it applies only to petition circulators.
